title: String::from_utf8_lossy in extract_headers is correct for message tolerance
description: extract_headers uses String::from_utf8_lossy rather than from_utf8 or a strict UTF-8 parse. A reviewer might suggest strict UTF-8 for correctness. That is wrong for this use case: real-world email messages frequently contain non-UTF-8 bytes in headers (Latin-1 in old messages, MIME-encoded words not yet decoded). The function's job is best-effort header extraction for matching, not validation. Replacing invalid bytes with U+FFFD is the correct maximally-tolerant approach. The strict UTF-8 check at compile() time applies to the Sieve script itself, which is appropriate. These two different tolerance levels are both correct.
